summary: Lead accepted M3-APPOINTMENT-ENFEOFFMENT-001 after systems_architect ACCEPT on R2 and independent lead gates. The accepted change adds versioned M3 appointment, bulk appointment, enfeoffment, office policy, jurisdiction policy, and decision scaffold command/query paths across protocol and sim-core. R1 review requested fixes for bulk collective primary-office conflicts and unknown enfeoffment granting polity authority; the original simulation_engineer fixed both in R2 with regression coverage, and systems_architect accepted the R2 behavior. The diff is confined to allowed sim-core/protocol/test/process paths and does not modify client UI, Electron, content packages, save-format, formal historical content, ADRs, gate docs, roadmap, checksums, or validation reports.

decisions: Lead accepts the systems_architect R2 ACCEPT review and marks M3-APPOINTMENT-ENFEOFFMENT-001 ACCEPTED, pending PR integration before CLOSED.
Player and AI actors use the same versioned GameCommand families for appointment, bulk appointment, policy update, and enfeoffment paths.
Bulk appointment remains deterministic and atomic: invalid batches reject before mutation with stable per-item reason strings sorted by itemId.
Authority remains state-derived from M3 polity or held office rows, not UI state.
M3 save/load remains intentionally unsupported by the existing M3 save refusal guard; this task did not touch packages/save-format.
Next dependency-ready M3 tasks remain visible, but autonomous execution must wait until this task is integrated and closed on main.
risks: Richer simultaneous appointment semantics, AI planning, diplomacy, war-loop integration, and client appointment UI remain separate M3 tasks.
M3 save/load remains intentionally unsupported until the scheduled M3 save-runtime task.
requested_action: Create the feature commit and PR for M3-APPOINTMENT-ENFEOFFMENT-001. After the PR is merged into main and verified, close the task in a separate closure branch. Do not start the next M3 task before integration.
